notes: Knockout bracket mapping for 48-team FIFA 2026 is not fully specified in the prompt; I used a best-effort allocation consistent with the standard 12-group format (A–L): R32 pairs typically match group winners vs a runner-up/advancing 3rd-place while preserving same-group separation where possible, and I assigned the eight advancing third-placed teams into open slots to minimize early rematches.

roundOf32.reasoning: Mexico’s home-region edge and ball progression should create more sustained pressure than Ecuador’s transition-heavy threat.
Canada’s athleticism and home conditions can level the match, with marginal shootout edge from game-state momentum and crowd.
Switzerland’s compact defending and set-piece efficiency match up well versus Scotland’s direct, lower-ceiling chance profile.
Morocco’s defensive structure and transition execution are better suited to grind down a close contest into extra-time.
Brazil’s depth and multi-channel chance creation should overwhelm Australia’s conservative, set-piece-reliant attack.
USA’s pressing/transition game plus host advantage should produce slightly higher-quality chances than Japan can convert.
Germany’s control in possession and superior chance volume should keep Egypt pinned back and limit counters.
A stylistic stalemate is likely, but Ivory Coast’s physical edge and set-piece threat can carry through to a shootout win.
Netherlands’ stronger ball progression and box presence should outscore Algeria’s transition moments over 90 minutes.
Uruguay’s duel-winning and box defending can suppress Sweden’s set-piece-centric path to goals.
Belgium’s superior attacking talent and chance creation should decide a game Bosnia struggle to open up.
Spain’s sustained possession and press control reduce Iran’s counter/set-piece routes, making a narrow Spain win likely.
France’s depth and transition threat should edge a tight, tactical game against Croatia’s control-oriented midfield.
Portugal’s technical quality in the final third and deeper bench should tilt a high-intensity matchup late.
Argentina’s game control and elite decision-making in key moments give them the edge in a low-margin matchup.
Austria’s press can create chances, but England’s finishing depth and set-piece quality are decisive over 90 minutes.

roundOf16.reasoning: In a familiar-conditions derby, Canada’s transition pace can keep it level and their defensive game-state management can win on pens.
Two disciplined blocks likely cancel out, but Morocco’s counterattacking efficiency can decide extra-time.
USA’s pressure and crowd help, yet Brazil’s superior technical execution under press should create the higher-quality chances.
Germany’s structure in possession and counterpress should limit Ivory Coast’s transition outlets and produce consistent shot quality.
Uruguay’s box defense can drag it to a draw, but Netherlands’ depth and composure give them a slight shootout edge.
Spain’s ability to control tempo and limit transition shots can neutralize Belgium’s creators over a full match.
A high-level stalemate is likely, but France’s bench and direct runners can decide extra-time.
Two elite sides may cancel out in regulation, with Argentina’s big-game shot quality and composure carrying penalties.

quarterfinals.reasoning: Canada’s athleticism and home-environment edge can turn a balanced match late as Morocco’s deep-block workload accumulates.
Brazil’s talent keeps it level, but Germany’s tactical control and set-piece/defensive reliability can win the shootout.
Spain’s possession structure can limit Netherlands’ transition volume, making a narrow control-based win plausible.
France’s depth of elite attackers and ability to create in broken play can tilt a tight matchup against Argentina.

semifinals.reasoning: Canada’s run meets a ceiling against Germany’s possession control, pressing structure, and superior finishing quality.
Spain can control phases, but France’s transition threat and bench impact are more decisive as fatigue increases.

thirdPlace.reasoning: Spain’s ball control and technical security should manage game state better than Canada once emotional fatigue sets in.

final.reasoning: In a high-level final, France’s edge in match-winners and transition efficiency can be the difference in a tight scoreline.
